sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presents with a chronic history of back pain, exacerbated by sitting and standing, with a recent worsening of symptoms over the past month.
The pain is described as an electric toothache that radiates from the lower back down to the left leg, with a maximum severity of 13 out of 10.
The pain is worsened by coughing, sneezing, and walking, but improved by lying down.
The patient has a history of a fall 30 years ago, which may have caused a fracture or disk injury.
The patient's pain level is currently 6 out of 10.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of back pain and leg pain, with a recent MRI showing a disk herniation at the level of L4-L5 vertebrae with nerve root impingement.
The patient has tried NSAIDs in the past without relief and has undergone physical therapy.
Review of systems: The patient reports numbness and tingling in both lower extremities, with weakness in both legs.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient has a positive pain response to palpation at the L4 vertebrae, with pain also present with flexion and extension of the back.
The patient has 4 out of 5 strength in the left leg and 5 out of 5 strength in the right leg.
Reflexes are brisk and motor and sensory are intact in both lower extremities.
Test Results: MRI results show a disk herniation at the level of L4-L5 vertebrae with nerve root impingement.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has acute disk herniation at L4-L5 with nerve root impingement, causing lower back pain and leg pain.
Problem list: Disk herniation at L4-L5 with nerve root impingement; lower back pain; leg pain; numbness and tingling in both lower extremities; weakness in both legs.
Plan:
I recommended an epidural steroid injection to alleviate the inflammation and pain caused by the disk herniation.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the area.
The patient will see the physical therapist and return for follow-up in about a month to assess the effectiveness of the injection and physical therapy.
Follow-up: The patient will see me again in about a month to assess the effectiveness of the treatment plan.
